assertions.description: CSS.supports() recognises a property value using if() with a supports() condition and an else branch.
assertions.test: color: if(supports(color: red): red; else: blue)

assertions.description: if() accepts media() conditions for media-query-driven values.
assertions.test: color: if(media(prefers-color-scheme: dark): white; else: black)

assertions.description: if() accepts style() conditions, mirroring @container style() queries.
assertions.test: color: if(style(--theme: dark): white; else: black)

assertions.description: if() supports multiple semicolon-separated branches evaluated in order.
assertions.test: color: if(supports(color: lch(50% 50 0)): lch(50% 50 0); supports(color: lab(50% 50 0)): lab(50% 50 0); else: red)

assertions.description: An element using if() resolves to one of the branches when read via getComputedStyle.
if() is a value-level construct, not a property; CSS.supports must reject 'if' as a property name.
